formatted_address: 21106 Kenswick Meadows Ct, Humble, TX 77338, USA
pitch_rationale: The homes at this Humble, TX address display classic hip/pyramid roof forms common to Houston-area suburban construction. Shadow lengths cast from the eave edges onto the surrounding lawn measure approximately 20–30% of building width, consistent with a moderate 6:12 pitch rather than the shorter shadows of a 4:12 or the longer shadows of an 8:12. The ridge peaks are visibly elevated above the eave plane with well-defined slope faces, and the ridge-to-eave geometry shows the ridge sitting noticeably inward but not dramatically compressed — both hallmarks of a 6:12 slope. Regional norms for early-2000s Texas suburban tract housing strongly support 6:12 as the dominant pitch, and no steeply protruding dormers or unusually compressed roof planes are present to suggest deviation from this standard.
footprint_rationale: Target home identified as the multi-section hip-roof structure at the base of the cul-de-sac on Kenswick Meadows Ct, occupying approximately pixels (235,555)→(415,885). Roof is a complex hip style with no gable/rake ends visible. Two primary stacking sections were traced: an upper main block (~170×155 px = 36×33 ft ≈ 1,183 sqft) and a lower extension (~140×120 px = 30×25 ft ≈ 754 sqft), minus ~87 sqft for cutouts and alignment, yielding ~1,950 sqft plan-view footprint. Ridge segments counted across 3 runs (~47 px total = ~45 ft). Hip lines estimated at 10 sloped corners averaging ~22 px each (~115 ft). Two to three valley intersections where wings meet (~22 ft). Zero rake lines (fully hipped). Eave perimeter traced around all lower edges (~165 ft). Confidence is moderate-low due to roof complexity, shadow interference on edges, and uncertainty in exact lot/house boundary assignment from satellite view alone.
